content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-3901          322-6441           322-6444

December 12, 1983


Florence Balaj
P.O. Box 1042
C2, B/111
El Segundo, CA 90245

                                Re: Your Request for Advice
                                    Our File No. A -83-250

Dear Miss Balaj:

     Thank you for your letter of October 31, 1983.

     You are the treasurer for the Hughes Active Citizenship
Fund, a recipient committee composed of Hughes employees. You
asked whether the committee could use computer runs to report
its contributors of $100 or more rather than the Commission form
Schedule A, and you sent us a sample of the computer run format
that you would like to use.

     Several other committees have made similar requests
recently. In the December Bulletin, there is a discussion of
the use of computerized campaign statement (copy enclosed), and
we are in the process of preparing informational materials for
committees who wish to use computer printouts as a part of their
disclosure statements; these materials should be available by
mid-January. Helen Arriola of our Los Angeles office will be
coordinating the approval process.

     Based on the criteria that we have developed for the use of
computerized schedules, the only problem with the sample
Schedule A that you submitted is the use of abbreviations for
the names of cities (e.g. "Los" for Los Angeles). Abbreviations
will only be allowed if they are easily understandable without
reference to anything outside the schedule.

     Although we are in the middle of setting up a procedure for
the approval of computerized schedules, we will go ahead and
approve your committee's computer format for Schedule A as
submitted (without the abbreviations) now. When the

Florence Balaj
December 12, 1983
Page 2


informational materials are finished, we will send you a copy
and ask you to review them to ensure your schedule complies with
all of the guidelines. With the materials, we will be providing
a few samples of acceptable formats. If you wish, you could
defer your use of your proposed format until you have had a
chance to study the materials.

This approval of your proposed computer format does not
constitute approval of the computer program; you are still
responsible for ensuring that your computer program includes all
the information required by the Act. Any changes to the format
must be approved by the Commission before they can be
instituted.

Please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-6444, if you
have further questions.

Sincerely,

Diane Maura Fishburn
Counsel
Legal Division

DMF:plh

I.D. #741884

P.O. Box 1042
C2, B/111
El Segundo, CA 90245
October 31, 1983


Ms. Barbara Millman
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. Millman:

This is to request permission to use computer runs
to list contributors of $100. or more. It would facilitate preparing reports to use the runs.

Attached is a sample of the material I would use.
It gives the information supplied on Form 420, Schedule A, but
in a slightly different format.

Is this computer run format acceptable to use in the
reports required to file for our committee?

Sincerely,

Florence Balaj
(Miss) Florence Balaj, Treasurer
Hughes Active Citizenship Fund

Sample - not a report

SCHEDULE A

MONETARY CONTRIBUTIONS RECEIVED
FORM 420, 430 OR 490

(Amounts May Be Rounded To Whole Dollars)

STATEMENT COVERS PERIOD
FROM                THROUGH

NAME OF CANDIDATE OR COMMITTEE:                                                I.D. NUMBER (IF COMMITTEE)

Hughes Active Citizenship Fund                                                  741884

DATE        NAME AND ADDRESS OF                          OCCUPATION           EMPLOYER               AMOUNT
REC'D       CONTRIBUTOR                                                        (IF SELF-EMPLOYED, ENTER    RECEIVED    CUMULATIVE
            (IF COMMITTEE, ENTER I.D. NUMBER OR                                NAME OF BUSINESS)                        TO DATE
            TREASURER'S NAME AND ADDRESS)

            CONTRIBUTIONS $100. OR MORE,                 DETAIL ATTACHED      6,800.          6,800.
            

            

            

            

            

            

            

            

            If more space is needed, check box at left                                         SUBTOTAL    6,800.
            and attach additional Schedule A.

SUMMARY

1.    AMOUNT RECEIVED, $100 OR MORE (Include all Schedule A subtotals) . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . $ 6,800.

2.    AMOUNT RECEIVED LESS THAN $100 (Not itemized) . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .        500.

3.    TOTAL MONETARY CONTRIBUTIONS THIS PERIOD
      (Line 1 + Line 2) Enter here and on Line 1 Column B of Summary Page. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .    $ 7,300.


                                                    -3-

State of California

Memorandum

To     :     Barbara                                                    Date  :  11/4


From  :     FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
            Jeanne

Subject:    Hughes Active Citizenship Fund computer print-out

            I think the format is acceptable, however they must
        also include the employer of the contributor.

            I would also mention to them that if they intend to
        use computer print-out for any other schedules of the
        campaign statement, they must get approval on them.

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660         322-5901         322-6441         322-6444


November 7, 1983


Florence Balaj, Treasurer
Hughes Active Citizenship
Fund
P.O. Box 1042
C2, B/111
El Segundo, CA  90245
                                                Re: A-83-250

Dear Miss Balaj:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Diane Fishburn, an attorney
in the Legal Division of the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice
request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Chief, Legal Division

embedding.first_500_words: December 12, 1983 Florence Balaj P.O. Box 1042 C2, B/111 El Segundo, CA 90245 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A -83-250 Dear Miss Balaj: Thank you for your letter of October 31, 1983. You are the treasurer for the Hughes Active Citizenship Fund, a recipient committee composed of Hughes employees. You asked whether the committee could use computer runs to report its contributors of $100 or more rather than the Commission form Schedule A, and you sent us a sample of the computer run format that you would like to use. Several other committees have made similar requests recently. In the December Bulletin, there is a discussion of the use of computerized campaign statement (copy enclosed), and we are in the process of preparing informational materials for committees who wish to use computer printouts as a part of their disclosure statements; these materials should be available by mid-January. Helen Arriola of our Los Angeles office will be coordinating the approval process. Based on the criteria that we have developed for the use of computerized schedules, the only problem with the sample Schedule A that you submitted is the use of abbreviations for the names of cities (e.g. "Los" for Los Angeles). Abbreviations will only be allowed if they are easily understandable without reference to anything outside the schedule. Although we are in the middle of setting up a procedure for the approval of computerized schedules, we will go ahead and approve your committee's computer format for Schedule A as submitted (without the abbreviations) now. When the Florence Balaj December 12, 1983 Page 2 informational materials are finished, we will send you a copy and ask you to review them to ensure your schedule complies with all of the guidelines. With the materials, we will be providing a few samples of acceptable formats. If you wish, you could defer your use of your proposed format until you have had a chance to study the materials. This approval of your proposed computer format does not constitute approval of the computer program; you are still responsible for ensuring that your computer program includes all the information required by the Act. Any changes to the format must be approved by the Commission before they can be instituted. Please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-6444, if you have further questions. Sincerely, [signature] Diane Maura Fishburn Counsel Legal Division DMF:plh I.D. #741884 P.O. Box 1042 C2, B/111 El Segundo, CA 90245 October 31, 1983 Ms. Barbara Millman Legal Division Fair Political Practices Commission Post Office Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Dear Ms. Millman: This is to request permission to use computer runs to list contributors of $100 or more. It would facilitate preparing reports to use the runs. Attached is a sample of the material I would use. It gives the information supplied on Form 420, Schedule A, but in a slightly different format. Is this computer run format acceptable to use in the reports required to file for our committee?
